column1: # SOURCE: Estimated from general BWRO engineering literature and manufacturer guidelines
# DESCRIPTION: Treatment system efficiency multipliers by utilization band for BWRO plants. Models how energy consumption
column3: and membrane life vary with operating point relative to rated capacity. Sweet spot at 70-85% utilization where all multipliers equal 1.0.

column5: recovery_adjustment_pct (percentage points added to base recovery)

column1: # LOGIC: Below sweet spot — intermittent operation causes startup/shutdown cycling losses (higher specific energy) but gentler on membranes (longer life). Above sweet spot — higher transmembrane pressure increases fouling rate (shorter membrane life
column2: higher maintenance) and reduces marginal permeate flux (higher specific energy). Recovery drops at high utilization as operators run more conservatively to protect membranes.

column1: # DEPENDENCIES: Composes with water_treatment-research.csv — base parameters come from TDS band
column2: these multipliers modulate them by utilization ratio.

column1: # ASSUMPTIONS: Applicable to small-scale BWRO (100-500 m3/day). Hot arid environment. Multipliers are symmetric around sweet spot center (80%) with steeper penalty below than above for energy (startup losses dominate). Values are synthetic estimates suitable for educational simulation; upgrade to -research suffix when validated against specific plant performance data.
# DATA_QUALITY: Toy-grade. Multiplier magnitudes are consistent with published BWRO operational guidelines but not calibrated to a specific installation.
